title: [Phase 8a] looper-api: axum REST server, auth, envelope
description:
REST API crate using axum. 31+ endpoints, auth middleware, SSE streaming, error response envelope.

Spec refs: module8-api-webhook.md (API sections), module-testing-strategy.md (golden file tests)

Key types: Handler (with RuntimeState trait), Server, Envelope<T> { ok, data, error, request_id }, ErrorInfo { code, message, details }, ErrorCode enum (19 variants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE)

Endpoints:
- Static: /webhook/forward, /api/v1/healthz, /api/v1/status, /api/v1/version, /api/v1/config, /api/v1/webhook/status, /api/v1/events, /api/v1/pull-requests, /api/v1/projects, /api/v1/loops, /api/v1/workers, /api/v1/planners, /api/v1/runs, /api/v1/runs/reconcile-stale (POST), /api/v1/runs/active, /api/v1/reviewer/repair
- Dynamic: /api/v1/loops/{id}, /api/v1/loops/{id}/logs/follow (SSE), /api/v1/projects/{id}, /api/v1/events/{type}/{id}, /api/v1/pull-requests/{id}, /api/v1/runs/active/{id}, /api/v1/runs/{id}, /api/v1/loops/{id}/pause, /api/v1/loops/{id}/start, /api/v1/loops/{id}/retry, /api/v1/loops/{id}/stop, /api/v1/loops/{id}/close

Auth middleware: authorizeRequest() — loopback exception for webhook, Bearer token match for API, auth_mode none for unrestricted. 19 ErrorCode values with HTTP status mapping.

SSE: channel subscription model, client disconnect handling, backpressure.

Dep: looper-types, looper-config, looper-storage, looper-service, looper-scheduler

title: [Phase 1] looper-types: Domain enums, state machines, core types
description:
Implement the zero-dependency domain types crate. This is the foundation crate that ALL other crates depend on. It defines pure types with no IO.

**Spec refs:** module-domain-state-machine.md, module1-config-types.md (enum parts only)

**Key Rust types:**
- `LoopType` enum (planner, reviewer, worker, fixer)
- `LoopTargetType` enum (project, pull_request, issue)
- `LoopStatus` enum (10 states: idle, queued, running, paused, waiting, stopped, terminated, completed, failed, interrupted) with transition method `can_transition_to() -> bool`
- `RunStatus` enum (7 states: queued, running, success, failed, cancelled, interrupted, parse_failed) with transition method
- `LoopTarget` struct with `target_key()` method
- `ResumePolicy` enum (6 values: replay_step, retry_from_timeout_context, advance_from_checkpoint, restart_from_discover, manual_intervention, rerun_review)
- `FailureKind` enum (retryable_transient, retryable_after_resume, non_retryable, manual_intervention)
- `FailureBoundary` enum (10+ boundaries: git_remote, git_local, github_api, model_provider, agent_process, local_worktree, storage, config, checkpoint, policy, unknown)
- Agent vendor enums, log level, auth mode, all string enums from config types
- Config structs (ServerConfig, StorageConfig, SchedulerConfig, etc.) — pure data types only
- `PartialConfig` — all-Option version of Config for merge
- `LoopSummary`, `StepName` validation
- `AgentExecutionRecord`, `EventLogRecord`, `QueueItemRecord` — core record types

**Behaviors to implement:**
- `LoopStatus::can_transition_to(target)` — compile-time safe transition matrix (25 valid transitions)
- `RunStatus::can_transition_to(target)` — 7 valid transitions
- `LoopTarget::target_key()` — format: "project:{id}", "issue:{repo}:{number}", "pr:{repo}:{number}"
- `PRLockKey(repo, pr_number)` — "pr:{repo}:{number}"
- `assert_loop_type_matches_target()` — planner→issue, reviewer/fixer→PR, worker→any
- `is_active_loop_status()`, `is_conflicting_loop_status()`, `is_terminal_run_status()`
- `Steps::for_loop_type()` — returns step list for planner/reviewer/fixer/worker

**Test specs:** module-testing-strategy.md (Layer 1 — Unit tests)

**Verification:** cargo test passes with 100% state machine tests

title: [Phase 2] looper-config: Config loading, validation, merge, disclosure
description:
Config loading crate — 3-layer merge pipeline: defaults → file (TOML/YAML/JSON) → env vars → CLI flags.

**Spec refs:** module1-config-types.md (full), module-security-concurrency.md (env sanitization)

**Key types:**
- `Config` (top-level) — all sub-configs merged
- `PartialConfig` — all-Option version for incremental merge
- `ConfigLoader` — builder pattern, load from file/env/cli
- `ConfigValidationError` — field-level issues list
- `ValidationIssue { path, message }`
- `LoadFileMetadata { config_path, config_file_present, tool_detection }`
- `Stamper` — disclosure stamp builder

**Behaviors:**
- `resolve_config_path()` — search ~/.looper/config.{toml,yaml,yml,json}
- `load_file()` → merge defaults → file → env → CLI
- `validate()` — 300+ validation rules per field (port range, enum values, path writable, etc.)
- `normalize()` — apply defaults to partial
- `merge_config()` / `merge_*_config()` — deep merge for each sub-config
- `build_env_overrides()` — 50+ env var mappings
- Disclosure stamper: `stamp_commit_message()`, `stamp_markdown()`, `strip_markdown_stamp()`, `has_markdown_stamp()`
- `Stamper::from_config()` with version, agent, model info
- Protected instruction phrase detection (30+ blocked phrases)

**Dependencies:** looper-types for enums and struct definitions

**Tests:** 300+ validation rule tests, format roundtrip (TOML→YAML→JSON), env var mapping, disclosure stamping, mixed schema detection

**Verification:** All config formats parse correctly, validation catches all error cases

title: [Phase 5a] looper-github: GitHub gateway via gh CLI
description:
GitHub integration gateway wrapping gh CLI commands. Handles PR/issue operations, reviews, labels, webhooks, and GraphQL queries.

Spec ref: module3-github-gateway.md, module8-api-webhook.md

Key types: Gateway struct, GatewayOptions, PullRequestSummary/Detail, SubmitReviewInput, ReviewThread, Discovery caches

Behaviors: PR CRUD, issue CRUD, review submission (inline comments + anchor validation), thread resolution, label management, auth checks, discovery cache (30s TTL), transient error detection (50+ patterns), 50+ gh CLI command patterns

Key: Gateway::submit_review() — most complex: normalizes anchors, validates quality gates, handles disclosure stamps, submits via REST API with inline comments or gh CLI fallback

Dep: looper-types, looper-config

title: [Phase 6] looper-scheduler: Tick loop, queue claim, discovery, recovery
description:
Scheduler crate — runs the discovery and queue claim loops. This is the heartbeat of the daemon.

Spec refs: module6-scheduler.md, module-recovery-infra.md (recovery pipeline)

Key types: Scheduler (tick_interval, wakeCh, stopCh, ticker), ActiveExecutionRegistry, AsyncRunner trait, HandlerMap, QueuePriority constants

Behaviors:
- Two concurrent loops: main tick loop (configurable interval, default 30s) + claim pump (1s)
- Tick flow: pre-claim → project iteration (discovery per role) → post-claim
- Discovery per role: Planner (issues with looper:plan label), Reviewer (PRs with review requested), Fixer (PRs with fix items), Worker (issues with looper:worker-ready), Coordinator (triage + dispatch)
- Queue claim: compute available_slots, claim non-long-term-retry first, then long-term-retry
- Claim SQL: CTE UPDATE ... RETURNING with lock key + blocker exclusion
- Stale reconciliation: when slots==0, reconcile stale running runs
- Wake channel sources: webhook received, CLI action, recovery requeue, new project
- Recovery pipeline (5 phases on startup):
  1. Orphan agent cleanup (PID verification via ps)
  2. Expired lock release
  3. Stale run reconciliation (30min heartbeat threshold)
  4. Loop state normalization
  5. Reviewer auto-recovery (deferred background)
- Failure classification: 4 FailureKind × 10 FailureBoundary mapping
- Long-term retry bucket: attempts >= 5 in slow lane

Dep: looper-types, looper-storage, looper-service, looper-github, looper-git, looper-agent

title: [Phase 10] Cross-cutting: Observability, Error handling, Rate limiting, Security
description:
Cross-cutting concerns spanning all crates. These must be designed upfront but implemented incrementally with each crate.

Spec refs: module-observability-error-handling.md, module-security-concurrency.md, module-rate-limit-degradation.md

Observability:
- tracing crate ecosystem: tracing + tracing-subscriber + tracing-appender
- tracing::Span for request-scoped context, tracing::Instrument for async span propagation
- tracing-subscriber JSON format + env-filter
- Event log system with type-safe event types (enum, not string), correlation/causation IDs
- Optional metrics (feature-gated prometheus endpoint)

Error handling:
- Per-crate error types with thiserror
- Cross-crate error conversion via From impls
- FailureKind + FailureBoundary enums (from looper-types)
- Error propagation policy: never leak internals to API/CLI
- Error display: user-friendly vs debug-verbose

Rate limiting & degradation:
- RetryPolicy: max_attempts, base_delay, max_delay, multiplier, jitter
- Exponential backoff: baseDelay * 2^attempt capped at 300s
- Overload protection: max_concurrent_runs, slow lane for >=5 attempts
- Graceful degradation: what happens when GitHub down / git locked / osascript unavailable / disk full
- Circuit breaker consideration for GitHub API repeated failures

Security:
- Auth: Bearer token + localhost exception
- Config file permissions: check 0600, warn on world-readable
- Env sanitization: 16 unsafe Git vars stripped
- Shell: direct exec (no shell wrapper)
- Worktree safety: 7 path validation checks
- Secret lifecycle: LocalToken, NodeToken, JoinKey, AdminToken, WebhookSecret

Dep: ALL crates

title: [Phase 4] looper-service: Loop, Run, Project business logic
description:
Service layer orchestrating Loop, Run, and Project lifecycle operations. Core business logic.

**Spec refs:** module-service-layer.md, module-domain-state-machine.md (state transitions)

**Key types:**
- `LoopService` — CRUD + state transitions for loops
- `RunService` — CRUD + lifecycle for runs
- `ProjectService` — project management with discovery
- `ResumePolicy` enum (shared with looper-types)

**Behaviors:**
- `LoopService::create()` — validate unique active, alloc seq, event log
- `LoopService::transition_status()` — validate allowed transition, side effects per transition:
  - idle→queued: create queue item
  - running→paused: cancel active queue items
  - running→completed: cleanup, mark done
  - running→failed: set resume policy, event log
  - paused→queued: requeue items
  - Any→terminated: cancel ALL, cleanup, terminal
- `LoopService::pause()` / `terminate()` / `resume()`
- `RunService::start_run()` — atomic: validate loop → create run → validate step → event log
- `RunService::record_step()` — heartbeat + step validation
- `RunService::complete()` — finalize run, transition loop if needed
- OneRunningRunPerLoop enforcement
- `ProjectService::add_project()` — ID collision, repo detection, discover worktrees/PRs
- `ProjectService::remove_project()` — archive + terminate loops + cancel queue
- `ProjectService::sync_configured()` — reconcile config-defined projects with DB

**Dependencies:** looper-types, looper-storage, looper-config

title: [Phase 12] diffanchor: diff parsing and anchor validation
description:
Diff anchor parsing utility — used by GitHub gateway for review comment validation.

Spec refs: module3-github-gateway.md (diffanchor sections)

Key types: Index { ranges: Vec<Range> }, Anchor { path, line, side, start_line, start_side }, Range { path, side, start, end, excerpt, heading }, ValidationResult { valid, reason, location_text, quality_flagged }

Behaviors:
- parse(diff: &str) → Index — parse unified diff format into structured ranges
- Index::format_prompt_section(limit) — format ranges as prompt context
- Index::validate(anchor) → ValidationResult — check if anchor points to valid location in diff
- validate_top_level_location(body) → ValidationResult
- fallback_body/fallback_location — degraded behavior for invalid anchors
- normalize_side(side) → normalize LEFT/RIGHT

Dep: looper-types

title: [Phase 8c] looper-infra: Bootstrap, runtime lifecycle, notifications
description:
Infrastructure crate — daemon bootstrap sequence, runtime assembly, shell execution, notifications, worktree cleanup.

Spec refs: module-recovery-infra.md, module-observability-error-handling.md, module-security-concurrency.md

Key types: Runtime (assembles all services), BootstrapOptions, Logger, NotificationGateway, WorktreeCleanupService

Behaviors:
- Bootstrap: LoadConfig → ValidateToolPaths → EnsureWritableDirs → CreateLogger → StartRuntime → WaitForShutdown
- Logger: tracing-subscriber with JSON format + env-filter, file rotation (10MB, 5 files)
- Shell: tokio::process::Command with timeout, bounded output (256KB), SIGTERM→SIGKILL escalation, process group isolation, unsafe env vars stripped
- Runtime: assemble scheduler, webhook, service layer, active execution registry
- Notifications: in-app (DB upsert + event log) + osascript (AppleScript, sound levels, throttling 60s window)
- WorktreeCleanup: background loop (initial 1min, then configurable interval), Plan() with retention/loop/queue checks, safety validation
- Shutdown ordering: signal → API drain (1s) → scheduler stop → agent kill → webhook stop → storage close
- PID file + file lock for single instance enforcement

Dep: looper-types, looper-config, looper-storage, looper-service, looper-scheduler, looper-runner

title: [Phase 9a] looperd: Daemon binary entry point
description:
Daemon binary — thin entry point that wires everything together.

Spec refs: module10-cli-daemon.md (daemon sections), module-security-concurrency.md

The main binary. Bootstrap sequence: check --version/--help → load config → validate tool paths → ensure dirs → create logger → start runtime → wait for shutdown.

Signal handling: SIGINT/SIGTERM → graceful shutdown (API drain → scheduler stop → agent kill → storage close). 

PID file + file lock (fs2) for single instance enforcement.

Dep: looper-config, looper-infra, looper-storage, looper-service, looper-scheduler, looper-runner, looper-api, looper-webhook

title: [Phase 0] Scaffold: Cargo workspace + CI/CD foundation
description:
Create the Cargo workspace skeleton with workspace root Cargo.toml, rust-toolchain.toml, rustfmt.toml, clippy.toml, .gitignore, LICENSE (MIT), README.md, and .github/workflows/ci.yml + release.yml.

This is the foundation for ALL other crates. Must compile with `cargo check --workspace` even with empty crates.

**Spec ref:** LOOPER_RUST_DESIGN.md

**Subtasks:**
1. Create workspace Cargo.toml with 16 crate members, workspace deps (tokio, serde, clap, rusqlite, axum, git2, octocrab, etc.)
2. Create rust-toolchain.toml (stable, 4 targets: aarch64-apple-darwin, x86_64-apple-darwin, x86_64-unknown-linux-musl, aarch64-unknown-linux-musl)
3. Create rustfmt.toml + clippy.toml
4. Create 16 empty crate stubs (lib.rs / main.rs) so workspace compiles
5. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml (fmt + clippy + test, 3-OS matrix)
6. Create .github/workflows/release.yml (cross-compile 4 targets on v tag)
7. Create AGENTS.md with build/test/lint commands
8. Verify: `cargo check --workspace` passes

title: [Phase 5b] looper-git: Git worktree management
description:
Git worktree management via git2 crate + git CLI fallback. Worktree lifecycle, safety guards, protected branches.

Spec refs: module4-git-worktree.md, module-worktree-safety.md

Key types: GitGateway, WorktreeRecord, CreateWorktreeInput, CheckoutMode, WorktreeListEntry

Behaviors: Create (branch/detached) → Restore (from DB) → Prepare (fetch+reset) → Cleanup (remove+mark). Branch naming conventions, sanitize, protected branch enforcement. Push with --force-with-lease + remote head validation. 7 safety checks for path validation. Fetch lock retry (2x, 50-100ms).

Dep: looper-types, looper-storage, looper-config

title: [Phase 3b] looper-storage: Queue priorities + webhook state tables
description:
Queue priorities system and webhook forwarder/tunnel state storage — extension of looper-storage.

**Spec refs:** module2-storage-sqlite.md (queue_priorities), module8-api-webhook.md (webhook state)

**Key types:**
- `QueuePriority` constants (ordered: planner > reviewer > fixer > worker > snapshot)
- `QueueStats` struct (total_queued, eligible_queued, blocked_by_terminal_loop, blocked_by_lock_key, etc.)
- `QueueMarkRetryInput`, `QueueFailInput`
- `WebhookForwarderRecord`, `WebhookTunnelHookRecord`

**Behaviors:**
- Priority queue ordering in claim queries
- Long-term retry detection (attempts >= 5)
- Queue statistics computation
- Webhook forwarder CRUD (upsert, list, delete by repo)
- Tunnel hook management (ping tracking, orphan marking)
- Cleanup stale queued items

**Dependencies:** looper-types, looper-storage base

title: [Phase 7] looper-runner: Planner, Reviewer, Fixer, Worker, Coordinator
description:
The LARGEST crate — 5 runner state machines. Each runner is a step pipeline with checkpoint/resume.

Spec refs: module7-runner.md, module-coordinator.md, module-reviewer-criteria.md

Key types per runner:
- Planner: steps discover-issues → prepare-worktree → write-spec → publish → notify
- Reviewer: steps discover → filter → claim → snapshot → review → publish (+ sub-ops: worktree prep, thread resolution)
- Fixer: steps discover-pr → claim-pr → collect-fixes → prepare-worktree → repair → validate → push → reconcile-commits → resolve-comments → recheck
- Worker: steps prepare-work → prepare-worktree → plan → execute → validate → open-pr
- Coordinator: tick-based (not step-based) — triage, dispatch, depgraph, mergewatch

Shared behaviors:
- Checkpoint/resume: JSON checkpoint persisted after each step, ResumePolicy controls next action
- Step execution: persistStepStarted() → executeStep() → classify failure or persistStepCompleted()
- Deduplication: FindActiveByDedupe → CreateOrGetActiveByDedupe
- Failure handling: classify → failQueueItem → completeRun → updateLoop
- Spec PR lifecycle: labels (looper:spec-reviewing, looper:spec-ready, looper:needs-human), phases (Spec vs Implementation)

Coordinator sub-systems:
- Triage: ShouldTriage/ShouldReTriage guards, LLM-based triage decision, label management
- Dispatch: human-gated (slash commands /plan, /implement) vs autonomous (delay-based), dependency gate
- DepGraph: blocked-by chains from GitHub API, cycle detection
- MergeWatch: 8 action types classification (Merged, Conflict, RedCI, etc.), PriorWatchMarker, retry budget

Reviewer criteria: AcceptanceCriterion, Verdict, Evidence, DefaultVerifier, AutoMerge validation

Dep: looper-types, looper-config, looper-storage, looper-github, looper-git, looper-agent, looper-service, looper-scheduler

title: [Phase 11] linter: cargo clippy fixes across workspace
description:
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings and fix all warnings across the entire workspace. Use auto-fix where possible, manual fix otherwise. Ensure zero clippy warnings on all crates.

Dep: ALL crates (run as final step after all crate implementations)

title: [Phase 8b] looper-webhook: Webhook forwarder, event routing, tunnel
description:
Webhook forwarder receiving GitHub webhooks and routing to appropriate runner lanes.

Spec refs: module8-api-webhook.md (webhook sections), module-edge-cases.md (EC-021, EC-023, EC-072)

Key types: WebhookForwarder (queue with cap 128, 4 workers, TTL 1h), WebhookWorkItem, DeliveryStats

Behaviors:
- Event routing: pull_request → reviewer/fixer lanes based on action; pull_request_review → fixer; check_run → fixer; push → fixer as base_branch; issue_comment → ignore
- Routing logic: routeDelivery() — event type + action → lane(s)
- Project matching: case-insensitive FullName match
- Deduplication: delivery ID dedup (1h TTL) + work key dedup
- Worker pool: default 4 concurrent, retry up to 2x on transient errors
- Webhook lifecycle: startup (acquire daemon lock), reconciliation, shutdown (drain)
- Tunnel webhook management (webhook_tunnel_hooks table)
- Stats: deliveriesReceived, deduped, ignored, accepted, enqueued, coalesced, executions

Dep: looper-types, looper-config

title: [Phase 9b] looper-cli: CLI binary, 35+ subcommands
description:
CLI binary — communicates with daemon via REST API. 35+ subcommands for managing looper.

Spec refs: module10-cli-daemon.md (CLI sections), module-testing-strategy.md (golden file tests)

Command tree: status, project (list/add/remove), config (get/set/unset/show/edit/validate/migrate), daemon (install/start/stop/restart/logs/status), loop (list/inspect/failures/start/pause/retry), run (list/stats/reconcile-stale), pr (list/show/status), review, fix, work, plan, takeover, queue (stats/list/failed/cleanup), network (join/leave/status/members), webhook, netadmin, bootstrap, upgrade, version, ps, jump, logs, stop, close, retry, feedback, labels init, prompt preview, worktree cleanup

Output modes: human-readable (tables) + JSON (--json global flag)
API client: DaemonAPIClient wrapping all /api/v1/ endpoints with envelope parsing
Auth: Bearer token from config

Dep: looper-types, looper-config

title: [Phase 5c] looper-agent: Agent executor (5 vendors)
description:
Agent execution for 5 vendors with process lifecycle, native resume, 2-tier timeout, completion parsing.

Spec refs: module5-agent-executor.md, module-edge-cases.md (EC-030 to EC-036)

Key types: ExecutorConfig, RunInput, ExecutionResult (status: completed/failed/timeout/killed), AgentExecutionRecord (status: running/cancelling/completed/failed/interrupted)

Behaviors: Vendor arg construction (claude-code, codex, opencode, cursor-cli, hermes — exact flags). Native resume per vendor. Max_runtime + idle timeout with SIGTERM→SIGKILL. Completion marker __LOOPER_RESULT__= parsing (reverse scan). Env sanitization (16 unsafe vars stripped). Session ID extraction. Log persistence.

Dep: looper-types, looper-config, looper-storage

title: [Phase 3a] looper-storage: SQLite repositories, migrations, event log
description:
SQLite storage layer with 17 migrations, repository pattern, event log, and queue operations.

**Spec refs:** module2-storage-sqlite.md, module-edge-cases.md (EC-001 to EC-006)

**Key types:**
- `Repositories` container — all repo structs
- Individual repos: Projects, Loops, Runs, AgentExecutions, PRSnapshots, Events, Locks, Queue, Notifications, Worktrees, WebhookForwarders, WebhookTunnelHooks
- `MigrationRunner` — apply 17 embedded migrations via refinery
- `QueueItemRecord`, `ProjectRecord`, `LoopRecord`, `RunRecord`, `EventLogRecord`, `LockRecord`, etc.

**Behaviors:**
- Connection management: WAL mode, busy_timeout=5000ms, `_txlock=immediate`, pool size 4
- Migration runner: forward-only, embedded SQL files, schema version tracking
- Queue claim: CTE `UPDATE ... RETURNING` for atomic claim
- Lock acquire: `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE WHERE expires_at <= now`
- Event log: append with correlation_id, causation_id, actor tracking
- OneRunningRunPerLoop: partial unique index enforcement
- Queue dedup: unique partial index on dedupe_key WHERE status IN (queued,running)
- `WithTransaction` / `WithTransactionValue` helpers for atomic operations

**Dependencies:** looper-types, looper-config (for StorageConfig)

**Edge cases:** SQLITE_BUSY retry, migration version mismatch, ID collision, concurrent writes, large data growth

**Tests:** Migration sequence, repository CRUD, queue priority ordering, lock acquire/release, concurrent writes

title: [Phase 9c] looper-net: Loopernet cloud server binary
description:
Loopernet cloud server — multi-node coordination server for distributed looper deployments.

Spec refs: module9-network.md, module-rate-limit-degradation.md

Multi-node network coordination server. Manages node registration (join/leave), heartbeats, coordinator lease system, webhook forwarding.

Key types: CloudServer (axum), NodeRecord, CoordinatorLease, JoinKey, NodeCapabilities, NetworkClient

Endpoints: POST /v1/join, POST /v1/heartbeat, POST /v1/leave, GET /v1/status, POST /v1/coordinator-lease/{acquire,renew,handoff,expire,revalidate}, GET /v1/events (SSE), GET /v1/github/webhook-secret, POST /v1/github/webhook (HMAC auth)

Auth tiers: AdminToken (env LOOPERNET_ADMIN_TOKEN) + NodeToken (generated at join)

Lease: single "coordinator" lease per network, 30s TTL, fencing token for conflict detection

Client side: NetworkClient (join, heartbeat, leave, status, lease ops), Manager (heartbeat loop 10s, state persistence in network.json 0600, identity drift detection)

Dep: looper-types, looper-config
